title: Rich Smoky Ethiopian Beef Kitfo
description: A traditional Ethiopian dish featuring raw beef, smoked spices, and a side of peanut cookies, made ayurvedic-tamasic-friendly with careful ingredient selection. This kitfo is served on a tava griddle.
instructions: Prepare the mitmita spice blend by mixing together chili peppers, garlic, and spices.
Finely chop the beef tenderloin and set aside.
Heat the ayurvedic ghee on the tava griddle over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the ghee and saute for 1 minute.
Add the smoked paprika and mitmita spice blend to the ghee and stir for 30 seconds.
Add the chopped beef to the ghee and spice mixture, stirring gently to combine.
Serve the kitfo on the tava griddle, garnished with crushed peanut cookies and a squeeze of lemon juice.
Offer additional mitmita spice blend and lemon juice on the side for added flavor.
